_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L2 composited cavalry lancer (Outfitted Lancer: belt studs, spear-butt cap, iron-buckled bridle + browband, viewer-LEFT-hip pouch) and produce the L3 'Veteran Lancer' variant (Tier 1 top).
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Veteran Lancer beat = 4 visible adds (single steel pauldron cap on the spear-arm shoulder, leather grip cord wrapped diagonally around the upper third of the wooden spear haft, iron banding on the saddle pommel and cantle, knife sheath on the rider's anatomical-LEFT hip). Chained composite-only Rule 2 — image_urls[0] = L2 composited keeper, image_urls[1] = L1_Base scale anchor.

v2 (2026-05-06): rewrite — drop all 'anatomical-RIGHT/LEFT' language, switch to viewer-relative directional language as primary. NanoBanana / nano-banana-pro/edit interprets RIGHT/LEFT as viewer-relative even when prefixed 'anatomical-' (round-2 batch placed the pauldron on the viewer-RIGHT rein-arm shoulder — wrong side). v2 uses 'VIEWER-LEFT (spear-arm side)' / 'VIEWER-RIGHT (rein-arm side)' everywhere with no anatomical fallback. See Cavalry CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § D for the pipeline learning.

v3 (2026-05-06): pauldron lateral-placement bug persists — round-3 batch with viewer-relative language STILL placed the pauldron on the rein-arm shoulder (wrong side, same as round-2). v3 drops ALL directional words (RIGHT/LEFT/anatomical-) for the pauldron and rein/knife placements and replaces them with spatial-geometry anchors: 'same vertical column as the spear haft', 'directly above where the rider's hand grips the spear', 'NOT near the horse's head or mane', plus an explicit pixel-zone hint ('approximately 35-40% from the left edge of the canvas'). Also adds the contrastive geometric reference: the pauldron and the spear haft are on the SAME side of the rider's body, the rein arm and rein hand are on the OPPOSITE side.

v4 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — REMOVED the L3 knife-sheath positive add (AD: 'remove the dagger entirely and keep the pouch'). L3 now has 3 visible adds (pauldron on rein-arm shoulder, leather grip cord on spear haft, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle) — knife sheath dropped entirely. Negative block now explicitly forbids any knife sheath, dagger sheath, or second belt accessory. The L2 hip pouch on the spear-side hip remains as the ONLY belt accessory carried through the chain. Pauldron stays on the rein-arm shoulder per the round-3 directional-bias finding (CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § D).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy.

v5 (2026-05-07): drop explicit pauldron+saddle rivet language from positive adds + scrub stale L2 belt-studs PRESERVE/negative refs + add prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative (mirrors Infantry chain-origin guardrail; rivet bleed-through observed at Cavalry L3 generation).

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L2/composite/cavalry_L2_v2_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply THREE visible additive upgrades to the mounted cavalry lancer in the FIRST input image (the rider already wears the existing outfitted-lancer kit: an iron pommel cap on the wooden spear haft butt, iron-buckled bridle with an iron browband on the horse, and a small brown leather pouch hanging from the rider's belt on the SAME SIDE OF THE BODY AS THE SPEAR — the spear-side hip pouch). CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — the rider sits on the horse with the WOODEN SPEAR HELD VERTICALLY in one hand. In the input image, the SPEAR HAFT is the most prominent vertical line on one side of the rider, with the iron leaf-tip near the top of the canvas. The hand gripping the spear is the SPEAR-SIDE hand. The opposite hand grips the brown leather REINS at the horse's withers — that is the REIN-SIDE hand. The horse's head, muzzle, mane, and bridle are on the REIN-SIDE of the frame. The horse's tail and rump are on the SPEAR-SIDE of the frame. Throughout this prompt, treat 'spear-side' as the side of the canvas/frame/rider where the upright wooden spear haft is visible, and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side where the horse's head and the rein hand are visible. Do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement — use SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE only. (1) SINGLE STEEL PAULDRON CAP ON THE REIN-SIDE SHOULDER (the shoulder closer to the horse's head and mane, the shoulder the rein arm grips reins from) — add a small rounded neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) cap (~4 inches diameter) at the rider's REIN-SIDE shoulder, covering the shoulder where the REIN ARM meets the torso. The pauldron is on the SAME SIDE OF THE RIDER'S BODY AS THE REIN HAND. The pauldron is on the SAME SIDE OF THE FRAME AS THE HORSE'S HEAD AND MANE. The SPEAR-SIDE shoulder remains UNCOVERED at this level — only the brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket shows on the spear-side shoulder. (The matching second pauldron on the spear-side shoulder arrives at this level.) The pauldron is a single smooth domed disc of neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed), riveted at the lower seam to a warm-brown leather strap that runs diagonally across the chest under the surcoat from the REIN-SIDE shoulder down across the chest. The lower seam of the cap is a clean steel-on-leather joint with no decorative rivets visible. (2) LEATHER GRIP CORD WRAPPED DIAGONALLY AROUND THE UPPER THIRD OF THE WOODEN SPEAR HAFT — add a diagonal warm-brown leather cord wrapping the upper portion of the wooden spear haft where the rider's hand grips, in a tight diagonal spiral pattern with the cord width approximately three quarters of an inch, secured at each end (top and bottom of the wrap) with a small dark-iron pin or nail. The wrap covers approximately the upper third of the haft from the iron leaf-tip socket down past the gripping hand. The wooden haft below the wrap remains warm-brown wood. The iron pommel cap at the haft butt remains unchanged. (3) IRON BANDING ON THE SADDLE POMMEL AND CANTLE — add small dark-iron banding rings wrapping the front pommel of the brown leather saddle and the rear cantle of the saddle. The banding is matte neutral dark-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray), approximately half an inch wide. The saddle leather body itself remains warm-brown. The pommel is the small raised front of the saddle in front of the rider's body; the cantle is the rear lip behind the rider. Both gain the new iron banding. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: chiseled chibi jawline; confident flat closed-mouth expression; thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris; prominent slightly-hooked nose; warm peachy-tan skin tone with soft rosy blush; THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE covering the upper lip and curving down past the corners of the mouth approximately half an inch past each corner — handlebar-shaped, NOT bushy, NOT walrus, NOT extending into a beard; cheeks and chin CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble, NO five o'clock shadow; dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the kettle-helm. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the steel kettle-helm — neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed helm with a forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam, gentle sloping forehead curve, brim projecting forward over the eyes by approximately one and a half inches, open face. Smooth painted steel — NOT scale, NOT chainmail. THE RIDER TORSO: the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split SURCOAT — VIEWER-LEFT half mustard-yellow, VIEWER-RIGHT half crimson-red, vertical seam down the chest center, simple round neckline, hem reaches the natural waist — visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible. The brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket beneath the surcoat — both arms covered shoulder to wrist in matte warm-brown leather. The wide brown leather waist belt with a square brass buckle centered at the front. THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: brown leather long sleeves cover both arms shoulder to wrist; both hands bare warm peachy-tan skin (the hand on the VIEWER-LEFT side of the frame grips the wooden spear haft just above shoulder height — the hand now grips the new leather grip cord; the hand on the VIEWER-RIGHT side of the frame grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers); no bracers, no vambraces, no gauntlets at this level. THE RIDER LEGS: the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff (visible under the saddle skirt on the VIEWER-LEFT side); the chunky warm-brown leather knee-high riding boots reaching mid-calf, both boots in stirrups. THE WEAPON: the long wooden spear with iron leaf-tip — heavy warm-brown wooden haft running approximately six feet long, neutral gray (matching the existing kettle-helm tone, balanced gray) iron leaf-shaped spearhead at the top, held vertically in the rider's hand on the VIEWER-LEFT side of the frame at shoulder height with the spear-tip pointing UP. The iron pommel cap at the haft butt remains unchanged. The new leather grip cord wraps the upper third of the haft per Edit Goal item (2). THE REINS: the brown leather reins running from the horse's bit back to the rider's hand on the VIEWER-RIGHT side of the frame at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT with head and muzzle on the VIEWER-RIGHT side of the frame and rump and tail on the VIEWER-LEFT side of the frame, standing four-square with all four hooves visible at the bottom of the frame, neck arched, ears forward, single dark eye visible on the VIEWER-RIGHT side of the head with a bright catchlight. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Dark brown mane along the top of the neck approximately three inches long, dark brown tail extending behind the rump and falling to about hock height. The horse's pose, anatomy, solid-brown color, mane, and tail all remain identical in shape, color, and position to the input image. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle (now gains iron banding on pommel and cantle per Edit Goal item 3); the brown leather bridle with the EXISTING dark-iron browband across the forehead and the EXISTING dark-iron square buckles on the cheekstrap and throatlash (this bridle metalwork visible in the input image must remain). The bit (concealed in the horse's mouth) stays unchanged. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison draped over the horse's back covering rump, flanks, and chest with a red and blue checkered pattern (alternating crimson-red and steel-blue squares approximately three inches per square) and a steel-blue trim band along the lower edge, the lower edge cut into shallow rectangular tabs, draping to about knee-height — UNCHANGED, identical to the input image. THE EXISTING KIT: the eight dark-iron belt studs (4 each side of buckle); the iron pommel cap at the spear haft butt; the iron browband and iron square buckles on the bridle; the small brown leather hip pouch on the rider's VIEWER-LEFT hip with brass-stud flap closure — all four elements PRESERVED unchanged in shape, color, and position. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees toward the viewer's left so the rider's body presents to camera. The HORSE faces toward the VIEWER's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the VIEWER-LEFT side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the hand grips the wooden spear haft (now wrapped with leather grip cord) just above shoulder height; the spear stands roughly VERTICAL with the iron leaf-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas; the knuckles of the gripping hand visible to camera. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the VIEWER-RIGHT side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder; the rein hand is visible to camera holding the reins in a relaxed grip. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance: spear shouldered upright but not couched, reins held but not pulled. NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot, NOT a walk, NOT a gallop. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (~795px tall) and approximately 60.6% of canvas horizontal width (~621px wide). TOP of rider's helm at approximately 11.4% from top of canvas (~117px headroom). BOTTOM of horse's hooves at approximately 10.9% from bottom of canvas (~112px below). Rider+horse vertically centered. Approximately 21.6% empty margin VIEWER-LEFT, approximately 17.8% empty margin VIEWER-RIGHT. The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the iron leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves, DO NOT shrink the spear so its tip falls below the helm, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, minimal contact shadow under the horse's hooves, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the mustard-yellow and crimson-red split surcoat — the chest livery must remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest; DO NOT replace the surcoat with a body coat, gambeson, robe, mantle, or armor; DO NOT cover the surcoat chest with scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor at this level; DO NOT add scale-mail sleeves on the upper arms (those arrive at this level); DO NOT PLACE THE NEW STEEL PAULDRON ON THE SPEAR-SIDE SHOULDER (the shoulder where the upright wooden spear haft enters the rider's body from above) — the pauldron at goes ONLY on the REIN-SIDE shoulder (the side of the rider's body and frame closest to the horse's head, mane, and bridle); the SPEAR-SIDE shoulder stays UNARMORED at and only gains a matching pauldron at this level. DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory on either hip — the existing hip pouch on the spear-side hip is the ONLY belt accessory at and through every subsequent level; do NOT add a second pouch, tool-loop ring, leather tag, or any accessory hanging from the belt other than the existing single pouch. DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, cuirass, or any body metal armor; DO NOT add a closed helm, sallet, half-visor, full visor, helm crest, helm horns, or any structural addition to the kettle-helm; DO NOT remove the kettle-helm or replace it with a different helm; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT render any new metal piece in cool blue-gray steel, bluish steel, icy silver, chrome, OR overly warm/brown-tinted steel — every new metal addition must match the NEUTRAL gray tone of the existing kettle-helm and spear leaf-tip (balanced gray, NEITHER blue-shadowed NOR brown-shadowed; soft painted highlights that read clean-white, neither cool nor warm); DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or a five o'clock shadow — cheeks and chin stay clean-shaven; DO NOT change the surcoat colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color; DO NOT add metal sabatons, greaves, leg armor, or any leg metal at this level; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the spear; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the reins; DO NOT change the spear shape, the wooden haft length, the iron leaf-tip shape or size, or replace the wooden haft with a steel lance haft (steel haft arrives at this level); DO NOT add iron banding rings to the spear haft itself (those arrive at this level — at only the leather grip cord wrap is added); DO NOT change the orientation of the spear from vertical with tip pointing up; DO NOT add a pennon flag or banner to the spear (that arrives at this level); DO NOT change the brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket; DO NOT add bracers, vambraces, or gauntlets; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, or sword belt at this level — the new leather strap that supports the existing pauldron runs UNDER the surcoat across the chest and is only visible at the shoulder seam where it attaches to the pauldron; DO NOT make the new pauldron gold, brass, or any color other than neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed); DO NOT add gold trim, gold studs, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT remove or change any element — the iron pommel cap on the spear butt remains, the iron-buckled bridle with iron browband remains, the hip pouch on the spear-side hip remains; DO NOT add a second hip pouch or move the existing pouch; DO NOT add a sword, axe, mace, hammer, bow, dagger, knife, or any second weapon at this level; DO NOT change the brown leather saddle to a different color or material — the saddle leather body stays warm-brown with only the new iron banding added on pommel and cantle; DO NOT add a peytral, chamfron, crinet, flanchard, barding, or any horse armor at this level; DO NOT change the horse coat color from chestnut brown; DO NOT remove or change the white blaze; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT change the horse's mane shape, tail shape, ear shape, or neck arch; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, the steel-blue trim band, or the tabbed lower edge; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, lion, eagle, or coat-of-arms to the caparison or surcoat at this level; DO NOT change the chibi proportions of either the rider or the horse; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure or the horse; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane other than the existing minimal contact shadow; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
